description: Root epic for Launch Grid — SaaS company that creates and manages SaaS products. Owns GHQ.
title: Launch Grid

description: Core GHQ orchestration system and personal OS infrastructure
title: GHQ Core

description: Comprehensive audit of GHQ system to identify structural, content, and integration discrepancies
metadata: {"goal":"Identify all structural, content, and integration issues across GHQ","qualityGates":["Validate audit completeness"],"relatedSkills":["cleanup","garden"],"repoPath":"/Users/hassaansaleemmacbook2026/repos/ghq","successCriteria":"Complete audit report documenting all findings across Beads, Skills, Company isolation, Knowledge/qmd, and directory structure","workMode":"main"}
title: GHQ Integrity Review

description: As a GHQ maintainer, I want to validate the Beads task management system so that I can identify issues with task hierarchy, dependencies, and metadata
metadata: {"acceptanceCriteria":["All epic/task relationships validated","Dependency graph checked for cycles","Metadata schema compliance verified","Orphaned or dangling tasks identified"],"e2eTests":["Run bd list --type epic and verify hierarchy","Check bd validate output for errors","Verify all tasks have required metadata fields","List any tasks with broken parent/dependency references"]}
title: Audit Beads System Integrity

description: As a GHQ maintainer, I want to verify the SKILL.md migration is complete so that I can identify any orphaned skill.yaml files or broken skill references
metadata: {"acceptanceCriteria":["All skills migrated to SKILL.md format","No orphaned skill.yaml or registry.yaml files","All skill references in tasks updated","Skill auto-discovery working correctly"],"e2eTests":["Find any remaining skill.yaml files in .claude/skills/","Verify each skill has valid SKILL.md","Check for registry.yaml references in code/docs","Test skill discovery by listing available skills"]}
title: Audit Skills System Migration

description: As a GHQ maintainer, I want to verify company boundaries are properly enforced so that I can identify any cross-contamination of credentials, knowledge, or settings
metadata: {"acceptanceCriteria":["manifest.yaml accurately reflects all companies","Settings directories properly isolated","No credential leakage across companies","Company labels correctly applied to tasks/knowledge"],"e2eTests":["Verify companies/manifest.yaml is complete and valid","Check that companies/*/settings/ is in .claudeignore","Scan for any hardcoded credentials outside settings/","Validate company labels on all beads tasks"]}
title: Audit Company Isolation

description: As a GHQ maintainer, I want to audit the knowledge base and QMD index so that I can identify stale docs, missing embeddings, and content quality issues
metadata: {"acceptanceCriteria":["Embedding coverage analyzed","Stale/orphaned docs identified","Duplicate content flagged","Index health validated"],"e2eTests":["Run qmd stats to check embedding coverage","Use garden skill to detect stale/orphaned content","Check for duplicate knowledge files","Verify qmd search returns relevant results"]}
title: Audit Knowledge & QMD System

description: As a GHQ maintainer, I want to scan all GHQ directories so that I can identify unexpected files, naming violations, and structural anomalies
metadata: {"acceptanceCriteria":["All top-level directories scanned",".claudeignore compliance verified","Naming conventions validated","Unexpected files/directories flagged"],"e2eTests":["Scan .claude/, companies/, knowledge/, loops/ for anomalies","Verify no settings/ content is accessible","Check for files violating naming conventions (e.g., uppercase where lowercase expected)","List any directories not documented in CLAUDE.md"]}
title: Audit Directory Structure

description: As a GHQ maintainer, I want all audit findings compiled into a single report so that I can prioritize fixes and track system health
metadata: {"acceptanceCriteria":["All subtask findings aggregated","Issues categorized by severity","Report includes actionable recommendations","Baseline metrics captured for future audits"],"e2eTests":["Verify all audit subtasks completed","Report includes findings from all 5 audit areas","Each issue categorized (critical/major/minor)","Report saved to knowledge/ for future reference"]}
title: Consolidate Audit Findings

description: Create a deep-research skill that autonomously searches the web, reads multiple sources, and produces a comprehensive markdown report with citations — similar to ChatGPT's deep research feature
metadata: {"goal":"Autonomous web research skill that produces structured markdown reports with citations","qualityGates":["Link validation — verify cited URLs are accessible"],"relatedSkills":[],"repoPath":"/Users/hassaansaleemmacbook2026/repos/ghq","successCriteria":"Running /deep-research with a query produces a saved markdown report with structured findings and verified source citations","workMode":"main"}
title: Deep Research Skill

description: As a GHQ user, I want a well-designed deep research workflow so that the skill produces thorough, well-structured reports. Research ChatGPT deep research behavior. Design the prompt flow: query clarification → search plan → iterative search/read loop → synthesis → report generation. Define the markdown report template (sections, citation format, source list). Document design decisions.
metadata: {"acceptanceCriteria":["Research flow documented: query → plan → search loop → synthesize → report","Markdown report template defined with sections and citation format","Iterative search strategy defined (how many searches, when to stop, how to follow leads)","Output path handling designed (user-specified path with sensible default)"],"e2eTests":["Design document exists and covers all workflow phases"]}
title: Design skill architecture and output format

description: As a GHQ user, I want to run /deep-research with a query so that it autonomously searches the web, reads sources, and produces a comprehensive report. Create .claude/skills/deep-research/SKILL.md implementing the designed architecture. Include: query clarification phase, multi-step web search using WebSearch, source reading via WebFetch, iterative deepening when leads are found, citation tracking, markdown report generation, and save to user-specified path.
metadata: {"acceptanceCriteria":[".claude/skills/deep-research/SKILL.md exists and follows GHQ skill conventions","Skill uses WebSearch and WebFetch for autonomous research","Implements iterative search loop (not single-shot)","Tracks and formats citations with source URLs","Asks user for output path and saves markdown report","Report includes: executive summary, detailed findings, source list"],"e2eTests":["SKILL.md is valid and auto-discovered by Claude Code","Skill prompt references WebSearch, WebFetch tools correctly"]}
title: Implement SKILL.md with research workflow

description: As a GHQ user, I want verified citations in my research reports so that all sources are accessible. Add link validation step to the skill that checks cited URLs before finalizing the report. Then run an E2E test: execute /deep-research with a sample query, verify the output is a well-structured markdown report with working citations saved to the specified path.
metadata: {"acceptanceCriteria":["SKILL.md includes a link validation step before finalizing report","Dead/inaccessible links are flagged or removed from final report","E2E test passes: sample query produces markdown report with citations","Report is saved to user-specified path"],"e2eTests":["Run /deep-research with a sample query (e.g. a factual topic) and verify: (1) markdown file is created at specified path, (2) report has structured sections, (3) citations include URLs, (4) no dead links in final report"]}
title: Add link validation and E2E test

description: Enhance run-loop to detect unblocked subtasks and execute them in parallel via multiple sub-agents, respecting dependencies and avoiding file conflicts
metadata: {"goal":"Run-loop executes independent subtasks concurrently instead of sequentially","qualityGates":["qmd update"],"relatedSkills":["run-loop","execute-task"],"repoPath":"/Users/hassaansaleemmacbook2026/repos/ghq","successCriteria":"Running /run-loop on a task with independent subtasks executes them in parallel batches with max 5 concurrency, while blocked subtasks wait correctly","workMode":"worktree"}
title: Parallel execution of unblocked subtasks in run-loop

description: As a run-loop orchestrator, I want to build a dependency DAG from bd dep list for all subtasks and group them into execution levels (batches of independent subtasks) so that each level can run in parallel
metadata: {"acceptanceCriteria":["Reads all subtask dependencies via bd dep list","Groups subtasks into levels where level N+1 depends on level N","Identifies which subtasks within a level are independent and can run concurrently","Handles edge cases: no deps (all parallel), linear chain (all sequential), diamond deps"],"e2eTests":["Given a task with 4 independent subtasks, all 4 are grouped in one batch","Given a task with A->B->C chain, each is in a separate sequential batch","Given diamond deps (A->B, A->C, B->D, C->D), batches are [A], [B,C], [D]"]}
title: Build dependency graph and identify parallel batches

description: As a run-loop orchestrator, I want to detect when subtasks in the same batch might modify overlapping files so that I can serialize them instead of running them in parallel
metadata: {"acceptanceCriteria":["Uses subtask metadata (repoPath, scope, or description heuristics) to estimate which files each subtask will touch","When two subtasks in the same batch have overlapping file scope, one is deferred to the next batch","Logging indicates when a subtask was serialized due to overlap detection"],"e2eTests":["Two subtasks both targeting the same SKILL.md file are not run in parallel"]}
title: Detect file overlap to prevent parallel conflicts

description: As a run-loop orchestrator, I want to spawn multiple sub-agents in parallel (up to max 5) for independent subtasks in a batch, using Task tool with run_in_background, so that unblocked work proceeds concurrently
metadata: {"acceptanceCriteria":["Spawns up to 5 sub-agents concurrently using Task tool with run_in_background","Each sub-agent runs /execute-task for its assigned subtask","Orchestrator polls/waits for all sub-agents in a batch to complete before proceeding to next batch","Handles sub-agent failures gracefully (marks subtask blocked, continues others)","Respects ONE loop at a time rule (no nested run-loops)"],"e2eTests":["Run /run-loop on a task with 3 independent subtasks — verify all 3 spawn as background agents simultaneously"]}
title: Implement parallel sub-agent spawning with max concurrency

description: As a run-loop orchestrator, I want to extend state.jsonl entries to track parallel batch execution so that the audit trail clearly shows which subtasks ran concurrently
metadata: {"acceptanceCriteria":["Adds batch_id field to state.jsonl entries when subtasks run in parallel","Parallel subtasks share the same batch_id","Sequential subtasks get unique batch_ids","Existing sequential behavior still logs correctly (backwards compatible)","loops-schema.md documentation updated"],"e2eTests":["After parallel run, state.jsonl contains entries with matching batch_id for concurrent subtasks"]}
title: Update state.jsonl schema for parallel execution tracking

description: As a run-loop orchestrator, I want the run-loop command file updated to replace the sequential while-loop with a batch-parallel pattern so that the full parallel execution flow is integrated
metadata: {"acceptanceCriteria":["run-loop.md updated: section 5 loop changes from pick-one-execute to pick-batch-execute-parallel","Batch selection uses dependency graph from subtask 1","Overlap detection from subtask 2 applied before spawning","Parallel spawning from subtask 3 used for execution","State tracking from subtask 4 used for logging","Progress display updated to show N parallel subtasks in progress","Auto-reanchor runs after each batch completes (not after each subtask)","Context safety net counts batches, not individual subtasks"],"e2eTests":["Run /run-loop on a task with mix of independent and dependent subtasks — independent ones run in parallel, dependent ones wait"]}
title: Update run-loop command with parallel orchestration logic
